title: Fruit Juice on Upholstery
timeEstimate: 15–45 minutes with extraction between every pass
stainChemistry: Fruit juice on upholstery is a tannin stain plus a sticky residue. The pigment is anthocyanin (grape, berry, cranberry) or yellow-orange pigments (orange, mango, carrot); sugars (fructose, glucose) form a sticky crystalline residue when the juice dries, trapping the pigment against the fiber. Citric acid component briefly shifts pH but doesn't drive lasting dye damage on cotton/synthetic upholstery. Check the upholstery code (W / W-S / S / X) before any liquid touches the fabric.
whyThisWorks: Same extraction-beats-chemistry rule as red wine on upholstery, with one extra step: the sugar residue needs emulsification BEFORE the tannin spotter can reach the pigment. Cold flush dissolves most of the sugar; NSD pulls the residue; acid-side tannin spotter disrupts anthocyanin grip; 3% H₂O₂ oxidizes the color. Weighted dry towel extraction between every pass protects the batting.
defaultAssumption: Check the upholstery code tag first. W / W-S: proceed with water-based chemistry. S-only: dry-cleaning solvent, not a DIY. X-code: vacuum only. Fresh juice on W-code upholstery is a good DIY; aged or dark-anthocyanin juices (grape, black cherry) on light upholstery trend toward pro.

spottingProtocol.technique: Code tag, then blot, no rub
spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white cotton towel, firm pressure
spottingProtocol.instruction: Read the upholstery code tag. W / W-S: proceed. S-only: stop and use a dry-cleaning solvent instead. X: vacuum only. Then press a folded white towel straight down on the stain. Lift, move to a dry section, press again. Outside-in. Never rub.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cold water, damp cloth, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply cold NSD via DAMP cloth — press onto the stain, lift, rotate. Never pour. Immediately weighted-dry-towel on top 5 minutes to pull moisture back up. NSD handles the sugar residue AND starts lifting pigment. Repeat 2–3 cycles.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side tannin spotter by dropper on residual color. Never pour. Dwell 3–5 min, blot with a dry towel. Patch-test a hidden spot on any dyed or printed upholstery.
3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop on residual color. Check every 5 min for color break. Blot with dry towel immediately when lift is visible. One oxidizer at a time.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD damp cloth, heavy dry towel, weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD-damp cloth to clear residual chemistry. Then heavy DRY towel + book/pan weight for 30 min to pull moisture up from batting.
Fan the spot. Don't sit on it until bone-dry. Inspect next day for wicking — if a ring returns, batting is saturated and a pro is needed.

steps: 1. Read code tag. W / W-S: proceed. S: solvent. X: vacuum only.
3. Cold NSD via damp cloth — handles sugar + starts pigment lift. Weighted extract between passes.
4. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A) by dropper. 3–5 min.
5. 3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop on residual. 5–10 min, check every 5.
6. NSD-damp flush + 30 min weighted dry towel.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
warnings: Read code tag first — S/X codes can't take water
Never pour or pool liquid on upholstery — batting saturation = water ring
Never skip weighted extraction between passes
NSD first for juice specifically — the sugar matrix blocks the tannin spotter otherwise

homeSolutions.agent: Find the Upholstery Code Tag First
homeSolutions.instruction: Before anything else, find the care tag — usually under a cushion. W or W-S = water-based OK. S = solvent only (not DIY). X = vacuum only. If the tag says S or X, stop and call a professional. Flooding an S- or X-code fabric with water will ring the fabric permanently.

Press a thick white towel straight down on the juice. Lift, move to a dry section, press again. Work from the outside of the stain toward the center. Never rub — rubbing drives the juice into the batting underneath where you can't reach it.

homeSolutions.agent: Cool Water on a Damp Cloth — Never Pour
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cool water, wrung nearly dry. Press on the spot, lift, rotate cloth. Repeat. The sugar in juice is water-soluble and lifts quickly. DO NOT pour water on the upholstery — it goes straight through to the batting and rings the fabric as it dries.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + 3% Peroxide Solution (Whites and Colorfast Only)
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 teaspoon Dawn dish soap with 1 cup 3% hydrogen peroxide. Dip a white cloth, wring nearly dry, dab the stain — outside edges toward center. Let sit 5 minutes, blot with damp cloth. Peroxide oxidizes the anthocyanin pigment. Test a hidden patch first on dark or patterned upholstery.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia — toxic fumes. Skip this step on dark/patterned upholstery if the patch test lifts dye.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Dry Towel Between Every Wet Pass
homeSolutions.instruction: After each wet dab, press a thick DRY towel on the spot with a heavy book or pan for 20–30 minutes. This pulls moisture UP out of the batting before it dries into a water ring. This is the pad-protection step.
Point a fan at the spot. Don't sit on the furniture until it's bone-dry — several hours. Inspect next day in daylight. If a faint pink/orange ring returns, call a professional upholstery cleaner.

homeSolutions.agent: Escalate for Wicking, Dark Anthocyanin, or S-Code
homeSolutions.instruction: Call a pro if: the code tag is S or X, the ring wicks back, the patch test lifted fabric dye, or the juice was dark grape / black cherry / cranberry on light upholstery (high anthocyanin load often needs pro chemistry). Tell them: "Fruit juice on [W / W-S / code unknown] upholstery, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% peroxide + weighted towel. [Type of juice.]"

steps: 1. Find the upholstery code tag. W or W-S: proceed. S or X: stop, call a pro.
2. Blot straight down from outside to center. Never rub.
3. Cool water on a DAMP cloth — never pour.
4. Dawn + 3% peroxide solution, dab with damp cloth, 5 min. Patch-test first on colored upholstery.
5. Weighted dry towel 20–30 min between every wet pass.
6. Fan-dry. Inspect next day for wicking.
7. Escalate for S-code, wicking, dark grape/cherry anthocyanin, or plateau.
products: A heavy book or pan for weighted drying
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if upholstery code is S or X, if the stain wicks back as it dries, if patch test shows dye bleed, if the juice was dark anthocyanin (grape/cherry/cranberry) and plateau'd, or if two home rounds didn't clear it.
warnings: NEVER pour or pool liquid on upholstery — batting saturates
NEVER skip weighted extraction between passes
NEVER mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia
S-code and X-code upholstery are not DIY

neverDo: Never pour liquid on upholstery — batting saturates
Never skip extraction between wet passes
fiberSensitivities: W / W-S upholstery tolerates water-based chemistry at room temp
Silk / velvet: escalate even on W-code

materialWarnings: Weighted extraction is the pad-protection step

escalation.when: S-code or X-code upholstery, stain wicks back, patch test shows dye bleed, dark anthocyanin juice on light upholstery, or two home rounds without clearing.
escalation.whatToTell: "Fruit juice on [W / W-S / code unknown] upholstery, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% H₂O₂ + weighted towel. [Juice type.] [Ring wicked / plateau / code unknown.]"

contextQuestions: What does the upholstery code tag say?
What kind of juice — grape / cherry / cranberry (heavy anthocyanin) or orange / mango / apple (lighter)?
Is the upholstery light, dark, or patterned?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Grape / black cherry / cranberry juice on light upholstery = highest pigment load; budget 2–3 peroxide cycles or escalate earlier. Orange / apple / mango = lighter pigment, usually clears in one cycle.
